response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, having been wrongly convicted, might express confusion and disbelief. They could feel a mix of emotions, including anger and helplessness. The situation would likely leave them questioning the fairness of the legal system. The word "lead" might come up when discussing the evidence that was misleading. A silver lining, if any, could be the opportunity to prove their innocence.

SECTION 2  
Sir, I did not do this, and I am truly innocent. I cannot understand how the verdict was reached without proper evidence. A stranger approached me earlier, claiming to have seen me at the scene, but I have no memory of that. I will not give up, and I will fight to clear my name. I need your help to wrap up this injustice and find the truth.
